---
title: "Neighbourhood Crime Trend Map"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

This interactive map summarizes how offence rates changed from 2014 to 2025. Use the layer control to switch between offence types. Blue indicates decreasing rates, red indicates increasing rates, and white indicates relatively stable rates.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
from shared_viz import make_slope_map

# Assumed input files generated by EDA_Analysis_notebook or included in the github repo:
# - data/cleaned_crime.csv: cleaned wide dataset with hood_id, area_name, geometry
df = pd.read_csv("data/cleaned_crime.csv")

# - data/df_long.csv is a very large file so it is not included in the repo, but it is the long format dataset with hood_id, area_name, offence_type, year, rate_per_100k this dataset will be reproduced here.

# Keep identifier columns and rate columns only
id_cols = ["_id", "area_name", "hood_id", "population_2025", "geometry"]

# Keep only the ID columns that actually exist in df
id_cols = [col for col in id_cols if col in df.columns]

rate_cols = [col for col in df.columns if "_rate_" in col]

df_rate = df[id_cols + rate_cols].copy()

df_long = df_rate.melt(

    id_vars=id_cols,

    value_vars=rate_cols,

    var_name="offence_year",

    value_name="rate"

)

# Split offence_year into offence and year

df_long[["offence", "year"]] = df_long["offence_year"].str.rsplit("_rate_", n=1, expand=True)

# Clean up types

df_long["year"] = df_long["year"].astype(int)

df_long["offence"] = df_long["offence"].str.upper()

base_cols = [col for col in ["_id", "area_name", "hood_id", "population_2025", "geometry"] if col in df_long.columns]

df_long = df_long[base_cols + ["offence", "year", "rate"]]
```

```{python}

m = make_slope_map(df, df_long)
m
```
